summary: Ukrainian forces intensified UAV attacks on Russian rear areas, striking port infrastructure in Izmail and multiple checkpoints across the eastern front. Russia continued pressure on northern and southern fronts, reporting advances near Volchansk and urban battles near Krasny Liman, while also targeting Ukrainian gas infrastructure in Chernihiv. Both sides reported increased drone activity and new Ukrainian weapon developments, including a domestically produced guided aerial bomb.
key_points: Ukrainian UAVs hit Russian port facilities in Izmail, Odessa region, and struck vehicles and checkpoints in Zaporozhye, causing civilian casualties (Two Majors report).
Russian assault units advanced up to 300 m on the Volchansk sector, engaging in forested fights along the Volchya River (Two Majors report).
Ukrainian drones attacked gas infrastructure in Chernihiv region for the third consecutive day, with no casualties reported (UkraineNow/81309).
Russian forces reported urban battles near Krasny Liman, expanding control zones around Drobyshevo and Svyatogorsk (Two Majors report).
Ukrainian forces claimed drone strikes on three men in Aleishki, Kherson region, and injured the Skadovsk district head (UkraineNow/81303).
Russian air defense shot down 315 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian territory during the past night (tass_agency/376489).
Ukrainian development of a 250 kg guided aerial bomb ready for combat use, enhancing strike capability against fortified targets (DeepStateUA/23515).
Russian tactical aviation activity increased on north‑eastern and eastern directions, threatening frontline regions (kpszsu/62194).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian UAV strikes on Izmail port and multiple eastern checkpoints marked the most significant operational development, disrupting Russian logistics. Russian forces reported limited advances on the Volchansk sector and intensified urban fighting near Krasny Liman, while continuing attacks on Ukrainian gas infrastructure in Chernihiv. Both sides escalated aerial activity, with Russia downing 315 UAVs and Ukraine fielding a new domestically produced guided bomb.

points: Ukrainian UAVs struck Russian port infrastructure in Izmail, Odessa region, damaging facilities and targeting logistics nodes, as reported by Two Majors (Two Majors).
In Zaporozhye region, Ukrainian strikes hit vehicles and checkpoints, resulting in one civilian death and two injuries in the village of Markovo, Kursk district (Two Majors).
Ukrainian forces launched drone attacks on three men in Aleishki, Kherson region, and injured the Skadovsk district head, indicating expanded southern UAV operations (UkraineNow/81303).

A domestically developed 250 kg guided aerial bomb has completed testing and entered limited service, providing Ukraine with a new precision strike capability against fortified targets (DeepStateUA/23515).
Ukrainian units advanced in the Sumy direction, securing Zapselye and Ryasnoe villages, and reported progress near the Ulano and Bachevka areas (Two Majors).
Ukrainian artillery and UAVs targeted Russian rear areas in the Dnepropetrovsk direction, advancing northwest of Alexandrovgrad (Two Majors).
Ukrainian forces reported successful UAV interceptions over the Black Sea, with 20 Shahed drones detected approaching Odesa (mon1tor_ua/66830).
Russian assault units on the Volchansk sector advanced up to 300 m, engaging in forested combat along the Volchya River and near Volkhovka and Karaichnik (Two Majors).
Urban battles intensified near Krasny Liman, with Russian forces expanding control zones around Drobyshevo and Svyatogorsk, encircling the town (Two Majors).
Russian forces reported a private house strike in Markovo, Glushkovsky district, Kursk region, killing one civilian and injuring two (Two Majors).
In Belgorod region, an FPV drone detonated in Borisovka, injuring a man; earlier, two men were killed in the same village (Two Majors).
Russian tactical aviation activity increased on north‑eastern and eastern fronts, posing heightened threat to frontline areas (kpszsu/62194).
Russian forces claimed to have worked on port infrastructure in Izmail and targets in Kharkiv, indicating ongoing logistical repairs (Two Majors).
Russian air defenses reported shooting down 315 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian territory during the previous night (tass_agency/376489).
Ukrainian UAVs hit checkpoints and vehicles in Zaporozhye front, causing civilian casualties and disrupting Russian supply lines (Two Majors).
Russian units advanced ten sectors up to 300 m on the Volchansk sector, with ongoing firefights in forested areas along the Volchya River (Two Majors).
Ukrainian GUR “Artan” unit operated in western Stepnogorsk, supporting drone attacks on Kamyanka‑Dneprovskaya, injuring a civilian (Two Majors).
Russian forces reported urban battles near Krasny Liman, expanding control zones around Drobyshevo and Svyatogorsk (Two Majors).
Ukrainian forces advanced in Sumy direction, securing Zapselye and Ryasnoe villages, and pushing towards Ulano and Bachevka (Two Majors).
Russian artillery and aviation intensified targeting of Ukrainian positions in the Dnepropetrovsk direction, advancing northwest of Alexandrovgrad (Two Majors).
Ukrainian drones attacked gas infrastructure in Chernihiv region for the third day, indicating sustained pressure on energy assets (UkraineNow/81309).
Russian assault units engaged in firefights in Shostka district, Sumy direction, near Ulano and Bachevka, while Ukrainian forces advanced in Zapselye (Two Majors).
Ukrainian UAV groups from Belgorod entered Ukrainian airspace over the Sumy‑Kharkiv border, increasing northern aerial threats (kpszsu/62202).
Russian tactical aviation activity heightened on north‑eastern and eastern axes, threatening frontline districts (kpszsu/62194).
Ukrainian reports of reactive Shahed drones over Chernihiv and near Ripky indicate continued northern UAV incursions (kpszsu/62198).
Ukrainian forces reported advancing near Ryasnoe village in Krasnopil district, indicating pressure on Russian positions (Two Majors).
Russian forces reported attacks on Ukrainian positions near Volchansk, with artillery and drone support (Two Majors).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed near the Black Sea, but no direct northern front impact reported (mon1tor_ua/66830).
Ukrainian drone strikes in Aleishki, Kherson region killed three men and injured the Skadovsk district head, showing intensified southern UAV activity (UkraineNow/81303).
Russian FPV drone detonated in Borisovka, Belgorod region, injuring a civilian; earlier two men were killed there, indicating cross‑border drone threats (Two Majors).
Ukrainian UAVs reported over Odesa region, including 20 Shahed drones from the Black Sea approaching Odesa (mon1tor_ua/66830).
Russian forces reported work on port infrastructure in Izmail, Odessa region, suggesting defensive efforts on the southern maritime corridor (Two Majors).
Ukrainian attacks on gas infrastructure in Chernihiv region, though northern, affect overall southern supply lines (UkraineNow/81309).
Ukrainian UAVs reported near the Black Sea, with groups over the sea but far from Odesa coast, indicating potential future southern maritime threats (eRadarrua/92522).
Russian artillery and drone activity reported near Kherson, with civilian casualties from UAV strikes (Two Majors).
Russian air defenses shot down 315 Ukrainian UAVs across Russian territory during the previous night, reflecting intense aerial contest (tass_agency/376489).
Ukrainian UAV groups from Belgorod entered Ukrainian airspace over Sumy‑Kharkiv border, increasing cross‑border aerial incursions (kpszsu/62202).
Reactive Shahed drones were observed over Chernihiv and near Ripky, indicating continued Russian UAV threats in northern sectors (kpszsu/62198).
Ukrainian forces deployed a new 250 kg guided aerial bomb, enhancing strike capability against fortified targets deep behind enemy lines (DeepStateUA/23515).
Russian tactical aviation activity increased on north‑eastern and eastern fronts, posing heightened risk to Ukrainian frontline positions (kpszsu/62194).
Ukrainian UAVs conducted strikes on Russian rear areas, including Izmail port and checkpoints in Zaporozhye, demonstrating expanded UAV operational range (Two Majors).
Multiple UAV interceptions reported over the Black Sea, with 20 Shahed drones heading toward Odesa, prompting heightened coastal air defense (mon1tor_ua/66830).

key_developments: Ukrainian UAVs struck Russian port infrastructure in Izmail, Odessa region, disrupting logistics and causing civilian casualties (Source: @Two Majors).
Russian assault units advanced up to 300 m on the Volchansk sector, engaging in forested combat along the Volchya River (Source: @Two Majors).
Ukrainian drones attacked gas infrastructure in Chernihiv region for the third consecutive day, indicating sustained pressure on Russian energy assets (Source: @UkraineNow/81309).
Russian forces reported urban battles near Krasny Liman, expanding control zones around Drobyshevo and Svyatogorsk (Source: @Two Majors).
Russia's air defenses downed 315 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian territory during the previous night, reflecting intense aerial contest (Source: @tass_agency/376489).
Ukraine fielded a new 250 kg guided aerial bomb, ready for combat use against fortified targets (Source: @DeepStateUA/23515).
Ukrainian drone strikes in Kherson region killed three men and injured the Skadovsk district head, showing expanded southern UAV operations (Source: @UkraineNow/81303).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces maintain pressure on the eastern and northern fronts, with modest territorial gains near Volchansk and urban engagements around Krasny Liman, while leveraging increased tactical aviation to threaten Ukrainian positions. Ukraine counters with intensified UAV campaigns targeting rear logistics, port facilities, and energy infrastructure, supported by new indigenous precision weapons that could degrade Russian defensive depth. The high volume of drone activity on both sides raises the risk of collateral damage and escalates the aerial contest, potentially prompting heavier air defense deployments.
sections.regional_response: Western partners continue to supply advanced air defense and artillery, but Ukrainian officials warn of costly missile expenditures against inexpensive drones, urging faster development of indigenous solutions. Russia seeks to involve foreign firms, including U.S. companies, in exploiting occupied Crimea's resources, indicating a broader economic dimension to the conflict. Neighboring NATO states monitor cross‑border UAV incursions, especially from Belgorod, while maintaining heightened alert on their own airspaces.
sections.intelligence_notes: The emergence of a domestically produced guided bomb suggests Ukraine's growing self‑sufficiency in high‑tech weaponry, potentially shifting future engagement dynamics. Russian advances remain limited to tactical gains, but increased aviation activity may signal preparation for larger offensives on the northern and eastern axes. Persistent UAV attacks on critical infrastructure, such as ports and gas facilities, highlight the strategic focus on disrupting supply chains and civilian morale.
